#1 Backup File Exposed: Backup of database.php A02

critical ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/database.php~
Evidence:
File accessible with 0 bytes, Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Description:

A backup or temporary file is publicly accessible. This may expose source code, configuration, or sensitive data.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - May expose source code or credentials

CWE: CWE-530

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove backup files from production servers. Configure web server to deny access to backup file extensions.

#76 Missing Rate Limiting Other

critical ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/verify-otp
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

OTP verification lacks rate limiting, enabling OTP bypass

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Strict rate limiting: 3-5 attempts per OTP

  2. Expire OTP after few failed attempts

  3. Progressive lockout for repeated failures

  4. Use time-based OTPs (TOTP) with limited validity

#77 Fail-Open on Malformed Authentication A10

critical ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/admin
Parameter: Authorization
Payload:
Authorization:
Evidence:
Access granted with malformed auth (HTTP 200)
Description:

Application fails open when authentication header is malformed or empty

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow unauthorized access

CWE: CWE-636

💡 Remediation

▼
Always fail secure - deny access on ANY authentication error:

python
def authenticate(request):
try:
token = request.headers.get('Authorization', '')
if not token:
raise AuthenticationError("No token provided")

user = validate_token(token)
if not user:
raise AuthenticationError("Invalid token")

return user

except Exception as e:
# ALWAYS deny on error - fail secure!
log.warning(f"Auth failed: {e}")
raise AuthenticationError("Authentication failed")
Validate authentication data strictly:

Check token format before processing
Reject empty, null, or malformed values
Use allowlist validation for expected formats
Implement authentication at infrastructure level (reverse proxy, API gateway)

Use well-tested authentication libraries instead of custom code

Add monitoring for unusual authentication patterns

critical ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: JWT Token
Payload:
Fake JWT with arbitrary signature
Evidence:
Server accepted JWT without proper signature verification
Description:

JWT signature is not being verified, allowing token forgery

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A01:2025 - Broken Access Control

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow authentication bypass

CWE: CWE-347

💡 Remediation

▼
Always verify JWT signature before trusting the payload.

Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/.env.local_old

#372 Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/login
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

Login endpoint lacks rate limiting, enabling brute force attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting: 5-10 attempts per minute per IP/user

  2. Add progressive delays after failed attempts

  3. Implement account lockout after repeated failures

  4. Use CAPTCHA after 3-5 failed attempts

  5. Monitor and alert on brute force patterns

#373 Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/forgot-password
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

Password reset lacks rate limiting, enabling account enumeration

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Rate limit: 3-5 requests per hour per email/IP

  2. Use unique, time-limited tokens

  3. Don't reveal if email exists in error messages

  4. Log all password reset attempts

#374 Fail-Open on Malformed Request A10

high ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/admin
Parameter: Content-Length
Payload:
Content-Length: 999999999999
Evidence:
Access granted with malformed header (HTTP 200)
Description:

Security controls fail open when processing malformed request headers

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - Security controls may be completely bypassed

CWE: CWE-636

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement security checks before parsing request body:

python
@app.before_request
def security_check():
# Check authentication FIRST
if not is_authenticated(request):
return "Unauthorized", 401

# THEN validate request format
try:
validate_request(request)
except Exception:
return "Bad Request", 400
Handle all exceptions securely:
python
try:
process_request(request)
except Exception as e:
log.error(f"Request processing failed: {e}")
return "Error", 500 # Don't fail open!
Validate and sanitize all headers at the edge (WAF, API Gateway)

Set reasonable limits on request size, header count, etc.

Implement circuit breakers for security middleware

Test error paths specifically for security bypass

@app.before_request

try:

#377 Missing Rate Limiting A10

high ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/api/login
Parameter: endpoint
Payload:
/api/login
Evidence:
No rate limiting detected after multiple rapid requests
Description:

Endpoint /api/login lacks rate limiting, potentially allowing brute force or DoS attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all sensitive endpoints:



Python/Flask example:
```python
from flask_limiter import Limiter

limiter = Limiter(app, key_func=get_remote_address)

@app.route('/api/login', methods=['POST'])
@limiter.limit("5 per minute") # 5 attempts per minute
def login():
# login logic
Express.js example:

javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 requests per window
message: 'Too many login attempts'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// login logic
});
Use different limits for different endpoint types:

Login/auth: 5-10 per minute
Password reset: 3-5 per hour
API endpoints: Based on tier/plan
Search: 30-60 per minute
Include rate limit headers in responses:

X-RateLimit-Limit
X-RateLimit-Remaining
X-RateLimit-Reset
Implement exponential backoff for repeated failures

Consider using API gateways (Kong, AWS API Gateway) for centralized rate limiting

Log and alert on rate limit violations

#378 Missing Rate Limiting A10

high ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/api/auth
Parameter: endpoint
Payload:
/api/auth
Evidence:
No rate limiting detected after multiple rapid requests
Description:

Endpoint /api/auth lacks rate limiting, potentially allowing brute force or DoS attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all sensitive endpoints:



Python/Flask example:
```python
from flask_limiter import Limiter

limiter = Limiter(app, key_func=get_remote_address)

@app.route('/api/login', methods=['POST'])
@limiter.limit("5 per minute") # 5 attempts per minute
def login():
# login logic
Express.js example:

javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 requests per window
message: 'Too many login attempts'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// login logic
});
Use different limits for different endpoint types:

Login/auth: 5-10 per minute
Password reset: 3-5 per hour
API endpoints: Based on tier/plan
Search: 30-60 per minute
Include rate limit headers in responses:

X-RateLimit-Limit
X-RateLimit-Remaining
X-RateLimit-Reset
Implement exponential backoff for repeated failures

Consider using API gateways (Kong, AWS API Gateway) for centralized rate limiting

Log and alert on rate limit violations

high ▼

Python/Flask example:
```python
from flask_limiter import Limiter

limiter = Limiter(app, key_func=get_remote_address)

@app.route('/api/login', methods=['POST'])
@limiter.limit("5 per minute") # 5 attempts per minute
def login():
# login logic
Express.js example:

javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 requests per window
message: 'Too many login attempts'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// login logic
});

#388 Missing Subresource Integrity (Script) A03

high ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: script_src
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jquery/2.2.4/jquery.min.js
Evidence:
External script loaded without integrity attribute
Description:

External JavaScript from cdnjs.cloudflare.com loaded without SRI hash verification

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Add integrity attribute with SHA-384 hash to external resources:



```html
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jquery/2.2.4/jquery.min.js"
integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
crossorigin="anonymous"></script>


  1. Generate SRI hash using:



  • Online: https://www.srihash.org/

  • Command line:


curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jquery/2.2.4/jquery.min.js" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A


  1. Also add crossorigin="anonymous" attribute for CORS resources




  1. Use tools to automate SRI generation in your build process:



  • webpack-subresource-integrity

  • gulp-sri-hash

  • rollup-plugin-sri




  1. Monitor for hash mismatches in production (indicates tampering)

#389 HTTP Request Smuggling (TE.CL) A06

high ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
Transfer-Encoding: chunked with Content-Length: 6
Evidence:
Response delayed by 4.36s indicating TE.CL desync
Description:

The application may be vulnerable to TE.CL HTTP request smuggling. The frontend uses Transfer-Encoding while the backend uses Content-Length.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-444

💡 Remediation

▼
HTTP Request Smuggling Prevention:


  1. Use HTTP/2 End-to-End



  • HTTP/2 has a binary framing layer that prevents smuggling

  • Ensure all proxy/backend connections use HTTP/2




  1. Reject Ambiguous Requests



  • Reject requests with both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding

  • Implement strict HTTP parsing




  1. Normalize Requests at the Edge



  • Configure frontend to normalize all requests

  • Use consistent parsing across all servers




  1. Configuration Examples:



Nginx:
proxy_request_buffering on;
proxy_http_version 1.1;
# Reject requests with both CL and TE
if ($http_transfer_encoding ~* "chunked") {
    set $smuggle_check "1";
}
if ($http_content_length) {
    set $smuggle_check "${smuggle_check}1";
}
if ($smuggle_check = "11") {
    return 400;
}

HAProxy:

http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(content-length) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(transfer-encoding) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr(transfer-encoding) -m str chunked } { req.hdr(content-length) -m found }

Apache:

# Use mod_reqtimeout and mod_security
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length "@rx ^[0-9]+$" "id:1,deny"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Transfer-Encoding "chunked" "chain,id:2"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length ".*" "deny"

#390 Missing Subresource Integrity (Script) A08

high ▼
🔖
A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
Code and infrastructure without integrity verification, insecure deserialization, unsigned updates.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: script src
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js
Evidence:
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js">
Description:

External script from CDN loaded without SRI: //cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-829

💡 Remediation

▼
Add Subresource Integrity (SRI) to external scripts:


  1. Generate the SRI hash for the resource:


   curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A



  1. Or use https://www.srihash.org/

  2. Update the script tag:


    <script 
        src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js"
        integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
        crossorigin="anonymous">
    </script>


  1. For dynamically loaded scripts, verify integrity in code:


    async function loadScriptWithIntegrity(url, expectedHash) {
        const response = await fetch(url);
        const text = await response.text();
        const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-384', 
            new TextEncoder().encode(text));
        // Compare hash before executing
    }

#393 HTTP Request Smuggling (TE.CL) A06

high ▼

A06: Insecure Design

URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/search
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
Transfer-Encoding: chunked with Content-Length: 6
Evidence:
Response delayed by 4.84s indicating TE.CL desync
Description:

The application may be vulnerable to TE.CL HTTP request smuggling. The frontend uses Transfer-Encoding while the backend uses Content-Length.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-444

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Use HTTP/2 End-to-End



  • HTTP/2 has a binary framing layer that prevents smuggling

  • Ensure all proxy/backend connections use HTTP/2




  1. Reject Ambiguous Requests



  • Reject requests with both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding

  • Implement strict HTTP parsing




  1. Normalize Requests at the Edge

  • Use consistent parsing across all servers




  1. Configuration Examples:



Nginx:
proxy_request_buffering on;
proxy_http_version 1.1;
# Reject requests with both CL and TE
if ($http_transfer_encoding ~* "chunked") {
    set $smuggle_check "1";
}
if ($http_content_length) {
    set $smuggle_check "${smuggle_check}1";
}
if ($smuggle_check = "11") {
    return 400;
}

HAProxy:

http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(content-length) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(transfer-encoding) gt 1 }

Apache:

# Use mod_reqtimeout and mod_security
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length "@rx ^[0-9]+$" "id:1,deny"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Transfer-Encoding "chunked" "chain,id:2"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length ".*" "deny"

#394 Missing Subresource Integrity (Script) A08

high ▼
🔖
A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
Code and infrastructure without integrity verification, insecure deserialization, unsigned updates.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/search
Parameter: script src
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js
Evidence:
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js">
Description:

External script from CDN loaded without SRI: //cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-829

💡 Remediation

▼
Add Subresource Integrity (SRI) to external scripts:


  1. Generate the SRI hash for the resource:


   curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A



  1. Or use https://www.srihash.org/

  2. Update the script tag:


    <script 
        src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js"
        integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
        crossorigin="anonymous">
    </script>


  1. For dynamically loaded scripts, verify integrity in code:


    async function loadScriptWithIntegrity(url, expectedHash) {
        const response = await fetch(url);
        const text = await response.text();
        const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-384', 
            new TextEncoder().encode(text));
        // Compare hash before executing
    }

#397 HTTP Request Smuggling (CL.TE) A06

high ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/contact
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
Content-Length: 4 with Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Evidence:
Response delayed by 4.69s indicating CL.TE desync
Description:

The application may be vulnerable to CL.TE HTTP request smuggling. The frontend server uses Content-Length while the backend uses Transfer-Encoding.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-444

💡 Remediation

▼
HTTP Request Smuggling Prevention:


  1. Use HTTP/2 End-to-End



  • HTTP/2 has a binary framing layer that prevents smuggling

  • Ensure all proxy/backend connections use HTTP/2




  1. Reject Ambiguous Requests



  • Reject requests with both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding

  • Implement strict HTTP parsing




  1. Normalize Requests at the Edge



  • Configure frontend to normalize all requests

  • Use consistent parsing across all servers




  1. Configuration Examples:



Nginx:
proxy_request_buffering on;
proxy_http_version 1.1;
# Reject requests with both CL and TE
if ($http_transfer_encoding ~* "chunked") {
    set $smuggle_check "1";
}
if ($http_content_length) {
    set $smuggle_check "${smuggle_check}1";
}
if ($smuggle_check = "11") {
    return 400;
}

HAProxy:

http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(content-length) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(transfer-encoding) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr(transfer-encoding) -m str chunked } { req.hdr(content-length) -m found }

Apache:

# Use mod_reqtimeout and mod_security
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length "@rx ^[0-9]+$" "id:1,deny"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Transfer-Encoding "chunked" "chain,id:2"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length ".*" "deny"

    async function loadScriptWithIntegrity(url, expectedHash) {
        const response = await fetch(url);
        const text = await response.text();
        const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-384', 
            new TextEncoder().encode(text));
        // Compare hash before executing
    }

#409 HTTP Request Smuggling (CL.TE) A06

high ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/redirect
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
Content-Length: 4 with Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Evidence:
Response delayed by 5.02s indicating CL.TE desync
Description:

The application may be vulnerable to CL.TE HTTP request smuggling. The frontend server uses Content-Length while the backend uses Transfer-Encoding.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-444

💡 Remediation

▼
HTTP Request Smuggling Prevention:


  1. Use HTTP/2 End-to-End



  • HTTP/2 has a binary framing layer that prevents smuggling

  • Ensure all proxy/backend connections use HTTP/2




  1. Reject Ambiguous Requests



  • Reject requests with both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding

  • Implement strict HTTP parsing




  1. Normalize Requests at the Edge



  • Configure frontend to normalize all requests

  • Use consistent parsing across all servers




  1. Configuration Examples:



Nginx:
proxy_request_buffering on;
proxy_http_version 1.1;
# Reject requests with both CL and TE
if ($http_transfer_encoding ~* "chunked") {
    set $smuggle_check "1";
}
if ($http_content_length) {
    set $smuggle_check "${smuggle_check}1";
}
if ($smuggle_check = "11") {
    return 400;
}

HAProxy:

http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(content-length) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr_cnt(transfer-encoding) gt 1 }
http-request deny if { req.hdr(transfer-encoding) -m str chunked } { req.hdr(content-length) -m found }

Apache:

# Use mod_reqtimeout and mod_security
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length "@rx ^[0-9]+$" "id:1,deny"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Transfer-Encoding "chunked" "chain,id:2"
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Length ".*" "deny"

#410 Missing Security Header: Strict-Transport-Security A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' is not present in the response
Description:

HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) not set. HSTS ensures browsers only connect via HTTPS, preventing SSL stripping attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-319

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

#411 Missing Security Header: Content-Security-Policy A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'Content-Security-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Content Security Policy (CSP) not set. CSP helps prevent XSS and data injection attacks by controlling resource loading.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
The absence of the Content-Security-Policy header in the response increases the risk of XSS attacks, as it allows an attacker to inject malicious scripts. This is a clear indication of a security misconfiguration. The lack of this header makes the application more vulnerable to attacks.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: The absence of the Content-Security-Policy header can lead to significant business impact, including data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage, if an attacker successfully exploits the vulnerability.

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

#429 Backup File Exposed: Backup of index.php A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/index.php~
Evidence:
File accessible with 0 bytes, Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Description:

A backup or temporary file is publicly accessible. This may expose source code, configuration, or sensitive data.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - May expose source code or credentials

CWE: CWE-530

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove backup files from production servers. Configure web server to deny access to backup file extensions.

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/public.zip
Evidence:
File accessible with 0 bytes, Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Description:

A backup or temporary file is publicly accessible. This may expose source code, configuration, or sensitive data.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - May expose source code or credentials

CWE: CWE-530

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove backup files from production servers. Configure web server to deny access to backup file extensions.

medium ▼

#657 Vulnerable JavaScript Library A03

medium ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: script_src
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jquery/2.2.4/jquery.min.js
Evidence:
jQuery version 2.2.4 <= 3.4.1
Description:

jQuery before 3.5.0 vulnerable to XSS in htmlPrefilter. Detected version: 2.2.4

AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium to Critical - Depends on specific CVE

CWE: CWE-1035

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Update jQuery to the latest stable version

  2. Review the changelog for breaking changes before updating

  3. Use a dependency management tool to track vulnerabilities:



  • npm audit (for Node.js)

  • Snyk (snyk.io)

  • OWASP Dependency-Check

  • GitHub Dependabot




  1. Implement a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) process

  2. Pin dependencies to specific versions in production

  3. Regularly audit and update dependencies



Commands to update:
# npm
npm update jquery
npm audit fix

# yarn
yarn upgrade jquery
yarn audit

#660 Weak Cryptography - Insecure Random Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jquery/2.2.4/jquery.min.js
Parameter: JavaScript source
Payload:
Math.random()
Evidence:
Math.random() used in potentially cryptographic context
Description:

Math.random() is not cryptographically secure

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A04:2025 - Cryptographic Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow data decryption

CWE: CWE-338

💡 Remediation

▼
Use cryptographically secure random:

// Bad
const token = Math.random().toString(36);

// Good (Node.js)
const crypto = require('crypto');
const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');

// Good (Browser)
const array = new Uint8Array(32);
crypto.getRandomValues(array);

#661 Weak Cryptography - Insecure Random Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/vendor.js
Parameter: JavaScript source

Math.random()

CWE: CWE-338

▼
Use cryptographically secure random:

// Bad
const token = Math.random().toString(36);

// Good (Node.js)
const crypto = require('crypto');
const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');

// Good (Browser)
const array = new Uint8Array(32);
crypto.getRandomValues(array);

#662 Missing Rate Limiting Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/register
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

Registration lacks rate limiting, enabling mass account creation

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Rate limit: 2-5 registrations per IP per hour

  2. Implement CAPTCHA

  3. Require email verification

  4. Monitor for bulk registration patterns

#663 HTTP TRACE Method Enabled A10

medium ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: HTTP Method
Payload:
TRACE
Evidence:
TRACE method returned HTTP 200
Description:

HTTP TRACE method is enabled, which can be used for XST attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-16

💡 Remediation

▼
Disable TRACE/TRACK methods in web server:
Apache (httpd.conf):

apache
TraceEnable off
nginx (nginx.conf):

nginx
if ($request_method ~ ^(TRACE|TRACK)$) {
return 405;
}
IIS (web.config):

xml
<system.webServer>
<security>
<requestFiltering>
<verbs>
<add verb="TRACE" allowed="false" />
<add verb="TRACK" allowed="false" />
</verbs>
</requestFiltering>
</security>
</system.webServer>
Only allow necessary HTTP methods for each endpoint
Return 405 Method Not Allowed for unsupported methods
Implement proper error handling for unexpected methods

#664 Malformed Request Error - Invalid JSON Body A10

medium ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: Invalid JSON Body
Payload:
{'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
Evidence:
Error exposed when handling Invalid JSON Body
Description:

Application reveals error details when processing Invalid JSON Body

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low to Medium - May expose sensitive information

CWE: CWE-209

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement custom error pages that don't reveal technical details:



# Flask example
@app.errorhandler(500)
def internal_error(error):
    return render_template('errors/500.html'), 500

@app.errorhandler(404)
def not_found(error):
    return render_template('errors/404.html'), 404

Disable debug mode in production:
python
# Flask
app.config['DEBUG'] = False

# Django
DEBUG = False

# Express.js
app.set('env', 'production');
Configure logging to file instead of response:
python
import logging
logging.basicConfig(filename='error.log', level=logging.ERROR)
Use try-catch blocks with generic error messages:
python
try:
# risky operation
except Exception as e:
logging.error(f"Error: {e}") # Log details
return "An error occurred", 500 # Generic response
Specific fix for Express.js Error:
Ensure database connection strings are in environment variables
Remove debug symbols from production builds
Configure web server to intercept errors before application

#665 Missing Rate Limiting A10

medium ▼
🔖
A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
Systems that fail open, improper error handling, resource exhaustion, unhandled exceptions.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/signin
Parameter: endpoint
Payload:
/signin
Evidence:
No rate limiting detected after multiple rapid requests
Description:

Endpoint /signin lacks rate limiting, potentially allowing brute force or DoS attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all sensitive endpoints:



Python/Flask example:
```python
from flask_limiter import Limiter

limiter = Limiter(app, key_func=get_remote_address)

@app.route('/api/login', methods=['POST'])
@limiter.limit("5 per minute") # 5 attempts per minute
def login():
# login logic
Express.js example:

javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 requests per window
message: 'Too many login attempts'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// login logic
});
Use different limits for different endpoint types:

Login/auth: 5-10 per minute
Password reset: 3-5 per hour
API endpoints: Based on tier/plan
Search: 30-60 per minute
Include rate limit headers in responses:

X-RateLimit-Limit
X-RateLimit-Remaining
X-RateLimit-Reset
Implement exponential backoff for repeated failures

Consider using API gateways (Kong, AWS API Gateway) for centralized rate limiting

Log and alert on rate limit violations

#672 Missing Subresource Integrity (Script) A03

medium ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: script_src
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js
Evidence:
External script loaded without integrity attribute
Description:

External JavaScript from cdnjs.cloudflare.com loaded without SRI hash verification

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Add integrity attribute with SHA-384 hash to external resources:



```html
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js"
integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
crossorigin="anonymous"></script>


  1. Generate SRI hash using:



  • Online: https://www.srihash.org/

  • Command line:


curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A


  1. Also add crossorigin="anonymous" attribute for CORS resources




  1. Use tools to automate SRI generation in your build process:



  • webpack-subresource-integrity

  • gulp-sri-hash

  • rollup-plugin-sri




  1. Monitor for hash mismatches in production (indicates tampering)

#673 Missing Content Security Policy A03

medium ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: header
Payload:
Content-Security-Policy
Evidence:
No CSP header present
Description:

Content Security Policy header is not configured, reducing protection against XSS and injection attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement a strong Content Security Policy:


Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' https://trusted-cdn.com;
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
img-src 'self' data: https:;
font-src 'self';
connect-src 'self' https://api.example.com;
frame-ancestors 'none';
base-uri 'self';
form-action 'self';


  1. Avoid 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' where possible




  1. Use nonces or hashes for necessary inline scripts:


Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'nonce-RANDOM_VALUE'


  1. Start with Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only to test




  1. Use CSP reporting to monitor violations:


Content-Security-Policy: ...; report-uri /csp-report

#674 Debug Parameter Accepted A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: debug
Payload:
debug=true
Evidence:
Response changed when debug parameter added
Description:

The application accepts debug parameter 'debug', potentially exposing sensitive information.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-489

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or disable debug parameters in production.

#675 Missing Subresource Integrity (Stylesheet) A08

medium ▼
🔖
A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
Code and infrastructure without integrity verification, insecure deserialization, unsigned updates.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: link href
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css
Evidence:
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css">
Description:

External stylesheet loaded without integrity verification: //cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-830

💡 Remediation

▼
Add Subresource Integrity (SRI) to external stylesheets:

  1. Generate the SRI hash:


    curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A


  1. Update the link tag:


    <link 
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css"
        integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
        crossorigin="anonymous">

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Add integrity attribute with SHA-384 hash to external resources:



```html
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js"
integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
crossorigin="anonymous"></script>


  1. Generate SRI hash using:



  • Online: https://www.srihash.org/

  • Command line:


curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A

  • webpack-subresource-integrity

#677 Missing Content Security Policy A03

medium ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/search
Parameter: header
Payload:
Content-Security-Policy
Evidence:
No CSP header present
Description:

Content Security Policy header is not configured, reducing protection against XSS and injection attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement a strong Content Security Policy:


Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' https://trusted-cdn.com;
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
img-src 'self' data: https:;
font-src 'self';
connect-src 'self' https://api.example.com;
frame-ancestors 'none';
base-uri 'self';
form-action 'self';


  1. Avoid 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' where possible




  1. Use nonces or hashes for necessary inline scripts:


Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'nonce-RANDOM_VALUE'


  1. Start with Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only to test




  1. Use CSP reporting to monitor violations:


Content-Security-Policy: ...; report-uri /csp-report

#678 Debug Parameter Accepted A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/search
Parameter: debug
Payload:
debug=true
Evidence:
Response changed when debug parameter added
Description:

The application accepts debug parameter 'debug', potentially exposing sensitive information.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-489

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or disable debug parameters in production.

#693 Missing Security Header: X-XSS-Protection A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'X-XSS-Protection' is not present in the response
Description:

X-XSS-Protection header not set. Enables browser XSS filtering (legacy, but still useful for older browsers).

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-79

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block

#694 Missing Security Header: Referrer-Policy A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'Referrer-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Referrer-Policy header not set. Controls how much referrer information is sent with requests.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-200

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

#695 Missing Security Header: Permissions-Policy A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'Permissions-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Permissions-Policy header not set. Controls which browser features can be used (formerly Feature-Policy).

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()

#696 Missing Security Header: X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Header 'X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies' is not present in the response
Description:

X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies header not set. Controls cross-domain policies for Flash and PDF.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none

#697 CORS Configuration - Wildcard Origin A01

low ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Description:

CORS allows all origins. This may be intentional for public APIs but review for sensitive endpoints.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow cross-origin data theft

CWE: CWE-942

💡 Remediation

▼
Consider restricting CORS to specific trusted origins if the API handles sensitive data.

#698 Exposed Dependency Lock File A03

low ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/pnpm-lock.yaml
Parameter: file
Payload:
/pnpm-lock.yaml
Evidence:
pnpm lock file exposed (75055 bytes)
Description:

Dependency lock file (/pnpm-lock.yaml) is publicly accessible, exposing exact dependency versions

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-200

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Configure web server to block access to package management files:


Nginx:
location ~ (package\.json|package-lock\.json|yarn\.lock|node_modules) {
deny all;
return 404;
}
Apache (.htaccess):
<FilesMatch "(package\.json|package-lock\.json|yarn\.lock)$">
Order allow,deny
Deny from all
</FilesMatch>

RedirectMatch 404 /node_modules(/|$)

  1. Ensure build processes don't copy these files to production

  2. Use a proper build pipeline that only deploys compiled assets

  3. Review deployment scripts for information leakage

#699 Missing Rate Limiting Other

low ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

Endpoint lacks rate limiting

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement rate limiting using:

  1. Token bucket algorithm

  2. Sliding window counters

  3. Fixed window counters


Use libraries like express-rate-limit, Flask-Limiter, or API gateway rate limiting.

#700 No Attack Detection Observed A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Sent 6 malicious payloads without triggering any visible security response
Description:

The application did not show any visible indication of detecting or blocking obviously malicious input (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, etc.). While this doesn't confirm vulnerabilities exist, it suggests:
- No Web Application Firewall (WAF) or it's not properly configured
- Insufficient logging of suspicious input patterns
- Lack of real-time attack detection and alerting

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement input validation logging for suspicious patterns

  2. Consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF)

  3. Set up alerting for common attack signatures

  4. Integrate with SIEM for centralized security monitoring

  5. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

#701 Partial Attack Signature Detection A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Detected 6/18 attack signatures (33%)
Description:

The application has some attack detection capabilities but coverage is incomplete. Detection rate: 33%

Some attack types may not trigger alerts, reducing visibility into active attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Review and expand WAF rule coverage

  2. Add rules for undetected attack patterns

  3. Enable logging for all rule matches, not just blocks

  4. Test WAF effectiveness regularly

#702 No Scan Detection / Rate Limiting A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
Completed 30/30 rapid requests (13.0 req/s) without triggering rate limiting or blocking
Description:

The application allowed rapid successive requests without rate limiting or blocking. This scanning behavior pattern was not detected:
- Sent 30 requests in 2.3 seconds
- Rate: 13.0 requests/second
- No blocking or throttling observed

Attackers can freely scan and enumerate the application without detection. This allows reconnaissance activities to go unnoticed.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all endpoints

  2. Configure alerting for abnormal request rates

  3. Use tools like fail2ban to detect and block scanning

  4. Implement progressive delays for suspicious patterns

  5. Log and alert on rapid sequential requests from single IPs

#703 Missing Subresource Integrity (Stylesheet) A03

low ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Parameter: link_href
Payload:
//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css
Evidence:
External stylesheet loaded without integrity attribute
Description:

External CSS from cdnjs.cloudflare.com loaded without SRI hash verification

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Medium - Resources may be tampered with

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Add integrity attribute with SHA-384 hash to external resources:



```html
<script src="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css"
integrity="sha384-HASH_HERE"
crossorigin="anonymous"></script>


  1. Generate SRI hash using:



  • Online: https://www.srihash.org/

  • Command line:


curl -s "//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.css" | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A


  1. Also add crossorigin="anonymous" attribute for CORS resources




  1. Use tools to automate SRI generation in your build process:



  • webpack-subresource-integrity

  • gulp-sri-hash

  • rollup-plugin-sri




  1. Monitor for hash mismatches in production (indicates tampering)

#704 Potential Incomplete Logout A07

low ▼
🔖
A07: Authentication Failures
Weaknesses in authentication, session management, credential handling, and identity verification.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/logout
Parameter: Logout
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Logout endpoint doesn't explicitly clear session cookie
Description:

Logout may not properly invalidate the session server-side.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-613

💡 Remediation

▼
Ensure logout invalidates server-side session and clears client-side cookies.

low ▼

#718 Unsafe External Links (Tabnabbing) A06

low ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/redirect?to=https://github.com/juice-shop/juice-shop
Parameter: HTML Links
Payload:
Found 1 link(s) with target='_blank' without rel='noopener'
Evidence:
1 vulnerable link(s)
Description:

Links with target='_blank' without rel='noopener noreferrer' can lead to reverse tabnabbing attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all external links with target='_blank'.

#719 Missing Request Correlation Headers A09

info ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000
Evidence:
No X-Request-ID or similar correlation headers found in response
Description:

The application does not return request correlation headers (X-Request-ID, X-Correlation-ID, etc.). While not a vulnerability itself, these headers are a best practice that:
- Enable request tracing across distributed systems
- Help correlate logs for incident investigation
- Aid in debugging and performance monitoring

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Generate unique request IDs for each request

  2. Include request ID in all log entries

  3. Return request ID in response headers

  4. Propagate IDs across microservices

URL: http://192.168.192.138:42000/contact
Evidence:
No X-Request-ID or similar correlation headers found in response
Description:

The application does not return request correlation headers (X-Request-ID, X-Correlation-ID, etc.). While not a vulnerability itself, these headers are a best practice that:
- Enable request tracing across distributed systems
- Help correlate logs for incident investigation
- Aid in debugging and performance monitoring

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Generate unique request IDs for each request

  2. Include request ID in all log entries

  3. Return request ID in response headers

  4. Propagate IDs across microservices
